subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Big Rip
description: A quick, practical guide to The Big Rip.

hook.text: What if dark energy gets stronger over time? Eventually it could tear apart galaxies, stars, atoms—everything.

buildup.text: In this scenario, the expansion accelerates without limit. Bound structures are ripped apart one by one.

discovery.text: Robert Caldwell proposed the Big Rip in 2003. If dark energy increases, the universe has a violent end date.

twist.text: Galaxies would unbind first, then solar systems, then planets, then atoms—all shredded by expanding space.

climax.text: Current data slightly favors constant dark energy, making the Big Rip unlikely—but it hasn't been ruled out.

punchline.text: Space itself tearing matter apart. A dramatic end, still possible.

quiz.question: What would cause a Big Rip?
options: Dark energy increasing over time until it tears apart all matter
A massive black hole consuming the entire universe
Antimatter reacting with all remaining matter
